title: Hissing consonants
subtitle: Letters Ж Ц Ч Ш Щ

hook.text: You're about to meet five sounds that English doesn't have as single letters: Ж (the sound in 'measure'), Ц (a ts-sound), Ч (ch), Ш (sh), and Щ (a longer sh-ch hybrid). These five letters appear constantly in Russian — master them and you'll sound noticeably more natural.

why.text: These five consonants are grouped together because they're all 'sibilants' — they produce a hissing or hushing sound. Russian uses them far more than English does. Getting them right early means you won't develop bad pronunciation habits that are hard to undo later.

cards.title: Ш and Ж — the sh/zh pair
cards.body: Ш makes the 'sh' sound as in 'ship'. Ж makes the voiced version of that — the sound in the middle of 'measure' or 'treasure'. These two are a voiced/voiceless pair just like the B/P, D/T pairs you saw in lesson 1-3.

cards.title: Ч — the ch sound
cards.body: Ч makes the 'ch' sound as in 'chair' or 'cheese'. It's always soft in Russian — you can't make a hard 'ch' sound with this letter. It never sounds like 'k'.

cards.title: Ц and Щ
cards.body: Ц makes a combined 'ts' sound — like the end of 'cats' or 'bits'. It's a single letter, not two. Щ makes a longer, softer sound than Ш — somewhere between 'shch' and a long soft 'sh'. It's the hardest of the five to get right. The word борщ ends in Щ.

examples.meaning: a long soft 'sh' sound — like 'shch' in 'fresh cheese'

questions.explanation: Ш = SH. Its voiced partner is Ж (ZH).
чай (chay) = tea. Ч=ch, А=a, Й=short y.

questions.prompt: Ц makes the sound found at the end of which English word?
questions.explanation: Ц = TS — the combined sound at the end of 'cats', 'bits', 'nets'.

cards.title: Ж always stays hard
cards.body: Unlike most Russian consonants, Ж and Ш are always hard — they never soften before Е or И. This is a spelling rule exception you'll encounter later. For now, just know that жи and ши (not жы/шы) are the correct spellings, even though the vowel doesn't technically soften the consonant.

examples.meaning: to live — Ж stays hard before И
to sew — Ш stays hard before И

cards.title: Щ in everyday words
cards.body: Щ appears in some very common Russian words. The key is to make it softer and longer than Ш — let the tongue press wider against the palate. If you just say SH, native speakers will understand but you'll sound slightly foreign.

questions.explanation: Ж = ZH — the voiced partner of Ш (SH). Think of the 's' in 'measure'.
шаг (shag) = step / pace. Ш=sh, А=a, Г=g.
борщ (borshch) = borscht — the famous Russian/Ukrainian beetroot soup. Ends with Щ.
цирк (tsirk) = circus. Ц=ts, И=ee, Р=r, К=k.
каша (kasha) = porridge / kasha. К=k, А=a, Ш=sh, А=a.
Ш (SH) and Ж (ZH) are a voiced/voiceless pair, just like С/З or Т/Д.

rules: Ш = SH, Ж = ZH (voiced partner of Ш), Ч = CH (always soft), Ц = TS, Щ = SHCH (longer, softer than Ш)
Ж and Ш are always hard — they never soften before Е or И
Ж devoices to Ш at word end (like other voiced consonants)
summary.nextLesson: Next: the final 7 letters — Ь Ъ Ы Э Ю Я Ё — including the two signs that modify pronunciation rather than adding a sound.

questions.prompt: Ж sounds like the letter in the middle of which English word?
Ц makes a combined sound. What is it?
Which statement about Ж and Ш is correct?
options: They are always hard — never softened by following vowels
Ж is soft, Ш is hard
They are silent at word end
questions.answer: They are always hard — never softened by following vowels

questions.prompt: Щ differs from Ш in that it is:
